name: El Morral and Roques de Mallasén Trail
name_original: Vilafamés — Tour circular al Castillo y las Rocas de Mallasen
description: Hike a difficult 3.6-mile (5.8 km) loop in Vilafamés, exploring the historic castle and unique Mallasen Rocks with expansive views.
region_intro: Hiking around Vilafamés offers diverse landscapes characterized by hilly terrain, unique geological formations, and historic landmarks. The region features prominent rock formations like Rocas de Mallasén and the distinctive La Roca Grossa, a massive sandstone block. Trails often lead through areas with maritime pine and undergrowth, providing varied natural environments. The village itself, built with local pink sandstone, sits atop a hill, offering panoramic views of the surrounding Valencian landscape. Best hiking trails around Vilafamés The most popular hiking route is El Morral and Roques de Mallasén Trail, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores ancient rock formations with archaeological significance. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vilafamés Castle – View of Vilafamés Castle loop from Vilafamés, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail offers elevated views of the historic castle and surrounding village. Local hikers also love the Vilafamés Castle – La Roca Grossa loop from Vilafamés, a 1.9 miles (3.1 km) trail leading through the village and past the iconic La Roca Grossa, often completed in about 53 minutes. Hiking around Vilafamés is defined by hilly terrain, unique rock formations, and historic castle views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy village walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Vilafamés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Vilafamés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vilafamés?
A: There are 17 hiking trails around Vilafamés, offering a variety of experiences from easy village strolls to more challenging ascents through the hilly terrain.

Q: What are the main natural features I can expect to see on hikes in Vilafamés?
A: Hikes in Vilafamés frequently feature unique geological formations such as the striking &lt;strong&gt;Rocas de Mallasén&lt;/strong&gt;, known for their ancient cave paintings, and the iconic, gravity-defying &lt;strong&gt;La Roca Grossa&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also encounter diverse landscapes with wooded masses of maritime pine and significant undergrowth, all set within a hilly terrain that provides numerous panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Vilafamés?
A: Yes, Vilafamés offers several easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14712744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilafamés Castle – La Roca Grossa loop from Vilafamés&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.9 miles (3.1 km) long and leads through the charming village past the famous La Roca Grossa. This trail is generally completed in under an hour, making it ideal for a shorter outing.

Q: What kind of historical landmarks can I discover while hiking in Vilafamés?
A: Many trails in Vilafamés incorporate historical landmarks. The prominent &lt;strong&gt;Vilafamés Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, perched at the highest point of the town, is a frequent focal point, offering both historical context and elevated views. The region also boasts archaeological sites like the El Tossal Cave and the Estrets-Racó de la Rata Iberian Settlement, adding a rich historical dimension to your outdoor exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Vilafamés?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Vilafamés are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilafamés Castle – View of Vilafamés Castle loop from Vilafamés&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) loop offering excellent views of the historic castle and surrounding village.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Vilafamés?
A: The hiking trails in Vilafamés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique combination of stunning natural rock formations, the panoramic views from the hilly terrain, and the opportunity to explore the historic village and its castle.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Due to Vilafamés's elevated position, many trails lead to fantastic viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Rocas de Mallasén&lt;/strong&gt; offer spectacular views extending to Peñagolosa. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilafamés Castle – View of Vilafamés Castle loop from Vilafamés&lt;/a&gt; provide commanding vistas of the village, its castle, and the wider Valencian landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the unique rock formations of the area?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877402492/el-morral-and-roques-de-mallasen-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morral and Roques de Mallasén Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you directly through ancient rock formations with archaeological significance. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14712744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilafamés Castle – La Roca Grossa loop from Vilafamés&lt;/a&gt;, guides you past the iconic, gravity-defying La Roca Grossa.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vilafamés?
A: Vilafamés benefits from a superb climate, making it suitable for hiking year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, Vilafamés offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877402492/el-morral-and-roques-de-mallasen-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morral and Roques de Mallasén Trail&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, covering 3.6 miles (5.8 km) with significant elevation changes, exploring ancient rock formations and requiring good fitness.

Q: What other attractions are near the hiking trails in Vilafamés?
A: Beyond the village itself, you can find various attractions. For instance, you might explore mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/674680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Àgueda Needles Pass&lt;/a&gt; or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/504604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Michael on Pico Bartolo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1323360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Summit&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive views.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Vilafamés?
A: Yes, parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of Vilafamés village, providing convenient access to the various trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations.
tips:
El Castell is without a doubt an icon of this beautiful town listed as one of the 100 most beautiful towns in Spain and that is undoubtedly worth a visit!
Its old town stands out and strolling through its streets is a journey back in time to its Arab origins.

Just a few km from Vilafamés are these wonderful rock formations, product of time and erosion. They not only stand out for their shape but also for their natural and archaeological value, since cave paintings have been found in one of their shelters with the representation of a schematic human figure.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this circular tour?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult hike, requiring good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. The terrain is varied, potentially technical, and includes steep, narrow, and winding streets within Vilafamés village, as well as natural paths leading to the Mallasen Rocks. Some sections may be challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in the charming village of Vilafamés. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Given the narrow, winding streets, it's advisable to look for designated parking areas upon arrival.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll explore the historic village of Vilafamés, visit the impressive Vilafamés Castle with its panoramic views, and encounter the unique geological formations of the Rocas de Mallassén. The village itself is a historical marvel with medieval architecture.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions are not noted, the trail's difficult classification, varied terrain, and steep sections, especially within the village, mean it might be challenging for some dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit for a hard hike and keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas and near historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circular tour?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions on the varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for hiking this circular tour or accessing the natural areas. However, there might be an entrance fee if you wish to enter the Vilafamés Castle itself.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: Expect breathtaking views throughout the tour. From the Vilafamés Castle, you'll get panoramic vistas of the village and surrounding landscape. The Rocas de Mallassén also offer spectacular views, including the distant Peñagolosa mountain to the northwest. The route also passes through picturesque olive and almond groves.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camí Vell de Vilafamés a Borriol, Carrer de Sant Ramon, Carrer de Basilio, Carrer del Camí de la Serra, and Camí de la Triciola, among others, as you navigate the village and surrounding areas.

faqs.question: Is this circular tour suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a "difficult hike" with varied, potentially technical, and steep terrain, this tour is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, making it more suitable for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: What is the 'Roca Grossa' and is it on the trail?
faqs.answer: The 'Roca Grossa' (The Large Rock) is a prominent, massive reddish sedimentary rock in Vilafamés, known for appearing to defy gravity. While not directly on the main hiking loop, it's an iconic feature within the village. Local legend suggests touching it and making three wishes will grant at least one. It's worth a quick detour if you're in the village.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this difficult hike, you should bring sturdy hiking shoes with good grip, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers for the weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended for navigation, although the trail is circular.
